title: euphorbiaceae
intro: The euphorbiaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, also known as the spurge family, characterized by its diverse range of species that include spurge, poinsettia, and rubber trees. This family is widely distributed across tropical and subtropical regions of the world, with species often found in wet and dry environments. Euphorbiaceae is known for its unique features, such as the presence of milky sap in some species and a wide variety of growth habits. With over 3,000 species, the euphorbiaceae family is one of the largest and most economically important plant families.
